function.description: This function provides the absolute value of a given number. For instance, when you input abs(-24), the output is 24.
This function calculates the arc cosine of a given number. For example, when you use acos(0.25), the result is approximately 1.318116071652818.
This function computes the arc sine of a specified number. For example, if you input asin(0.25), the result will be approximately 0.25268025514207865.
This function calculates the arc tangent of a given number. For example, if you input atan(0.25), the result will be approximately 0.24497866312686414.
This function computes the arc tangent of a given pair of numbers. For example, when you use atan2(0.50, 1), the result is approximately 0.4636476090008061.
This function returns the nearest integer less than or equal to the specified number. For example, if you use floor(3.1415), the result will be 3.
Returns number rounded up to the nearest integer. Example:ceiling(0.25) result:1
Returns the cosine of number. Example: cos(0.25) result: 0.9689124217106447
Returns the cotangent of an angle. Specify the angle in radians. Example: cot(0.25) result: 3.9163173646459399
Converts angle number in radians to degrees. Example: degrees(0.25) result: 14.32394487827058
Returns the integer part of a division operation. Example: div(5,10) result: 2
Returns Euler’s number raised to the power of the given number. Example: exp(2) result: 7.389
Returns the natural logarithm of number. Example: ln(2) result: 0.6931471805599453
Returns the base 10 logarithm of number. Example: log(2) result: 0.3010299956639812
Returns the largest of the provided number.
Returns the smallest of the provided number.
Returns the constant Pi. Example: pi() result: 3.14159
Converts given number from degrees to radians. Example : radians(4) result : 0.06981317007977318
Returns the signum function of number, that is: 0 if the argument is 0, 1 if the argument is greater than 0, -1 if the argument is less than 0. Example: sign(0.5) result: 1.
Returns the sine of number. Example: sin(0.25) result: 0.24740395925452294
It displays the square root of a number. Example: sqrt(5) result: 2.23606797749979
It displays the square of a given number. Example: square(4) result: 16
Returns the tangent of number. Example: tan(0.25) result: 0.25534192122103627
power function returns 'number' raised to the power of 'another number'. Example: power(4,2) result:16.
Returns number rounded to integer by dropping digits after decimal point. Example:TRUNC(1.289,2) result: 1.28
Returns number rounded to the nearest integer. Example: round(4.4912,2) result: 4.49
Returns the Unicode of the first character of string. Example: ASCII('A') result: 65
Returns the character encoded by the ASCII code. Example: CHAR(65) result: A
This function combines two strings together. For instance, CONCAT('Beng', 'aluru') yields 'Bengaluru'.

function.signature: case when(position(${substring} IN ${string}))>0 then true else false end
function.description: This expression evaluates to true if the specified substring is found within the given string. For example, the expression you provided will result in true because the substring 'g' is found in the string 'Bengaluru'.

function.signature: case when(${string} like concat('%',${substring})) then true else false end
function.description: This expression evaluates to true if the given string ends with the specified substring. For example, when you use the expression provided: case when('postgres' like concat('%','res')) then true else false end, it results in true because the string 'postgres' ends with the substring 'res'..

function.signature: case when(${string} like concat(${substring},'%')) then true else false end
function.description: This expression evaluates to true if the given string starts with the specified substring. For instance, when you use the expression: case when('bengaluru' like concat('ben','%')) then true else false end, it yields true because the string 'bengaluru' starts with the substring 'ben'.
Returns the left most characters in a string. Example: LEFT ('Bengaluru', 4) result:Beng.
Returns the number of characters in text. Example: length('Bengaluru') result:9
Converts all characters in the specified string to lowercase. Example: LOWER('BENGALURU') result: bengaluru
Converts all characters in a string to uppercase. Example: UPPER('bengaluru') result: BENGALURU

Displays a string that is left padded with a specified text to a certain length. If length is less than the length of string, the result is truncated to length characters. Length must not be negative and padString must not be non-empty. Example: LPAD('Wellesley', 14,'Hills') result: HillsWellesley

Displays a string that is right-padded with a specified string to a certain length. If length is less than the length of string, the result is truncated to length characters. Length must not be negative and padString must not be non-empty. Example: Rpad('Wellesley', 15, 'Hills') result: Wellesley Hills
Returns the rightmost character from the string. If length is negative extract all the characters from the right side except 3 leftmost characters Example: right('bengaluru',4) result: urul
Returns string with the characters in reverse order. Example: REVERSE('Bengaluru') result : urulagneB
Removes leading whitespace from string Example: LTRIM(' Bengaluru') result: Bengaluru
String search for substring and replace it with replacestring. If substring is not found, the string is not changed. Example: replace('bengaluru','b','Z') - zengaluru
Removes trailing whitespace from string. Example: RTRIM('Bengaluru ') Result: Bengaluru
Returns a string consisting of space characters. Example: rpad(' ',3,' ') result: .

Splits string on delimiter and returns the field index. Field indexes start with 1. If the index is larger than the number of fields, then null is returned. Example : split_part(‘a-b-c-d’, ‘-‘, 2) result: b, split_part(‘a|b|c|d’, ‘|‘, 2) result : b
Removes whitespace from string. Example:TRIM(' Bengaluru ') result: Bengaluru

Returns the specified date with the specified number of interval added to the specified date_part of that date.Example:date(date '2014-03-08' + Interval '2' year) result:2016-03-08 supported units:day, month, year.
Returns an integer that represents the specified datepart of the specified date. Example: date_part('year',date '2014-03-08'). result:2014supported date_part:YEAR, QUARTER, MONTH , WEEK , DAY.
Truncates the specified date to the accuracy specified by the unit. Example: datetrunc('year', date '2014-03-08'). result:2014-01-01 00:00:00.000
Displays Current date.
Returns the difference between date1 and date2 expressed in terms of unit. Example: DATE_PART('year', date '2014-03-08') - DATE_PART('year', date '2019-03-08');result: -5
Return hour for timestamp. Example: date_part('hour', timestamp '2014-03-08 12:20:19') result:12

Returns time value from the hour, minute and seconds.Example: case when length('12') greater and Less than 0 OR length('30') greater and Less than symbol 0 OR length('40') greater and Less than 0 then CAST(concat('12',':','30',':','40') as TIME) else null end Note : Replace greater and less than with greater than and less than symbols result: 12:30:40
Returns a datetime that combines a year,month,day,hour,minute,second. Example: make_timestamp(2013, 7, 15, 8, 15, 23.5) result: 2013-07-15 08:15:23.

Return a DATETIME format as a string. Example1: to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'YYYY-MM-DD HH12:MI:SS') result: 2014-03-08 09:00:00 Example 2 : to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'YYYY') result: 2014, Example 3 : to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'MM') result: 03, Example 4 : to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'DD') result : 08, Example5 :to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'HH') result : 09, Example6 :to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'MI') result :00, Example7 : to_char(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00', 'ss') result : 00
Takes a string as input and displays a date in a specific format. Example:to_timestamp( '2008-01-31 12:12:12'::string,'yyyy-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS.ff') result: 2008-01-31 00:00:00
Returns the difference between date1 and date2 expressed in terms of unit. Example:date_diff(year,timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00',timestamp '2019-03-08 09:00:00') result: -5

Returns the specified datetime with the specified number interval added to the date_part of that datetime. . Example: (timestamp '2014-03-08 11:10:27' + Interval '2' year) result:2016-03-08 11:10:27 supported units:day, month, year,hour,minute,second.
Returns an integer that represents the specified datepart of the specified . Example: date_part('year',timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00'). result:2014
Truncates the specified date to the accuracy specified by the unit. Example: datetrunc('year', TIMESTAMP '2014-03-08 12:20:19). result:2014-01-01 00:00:00.000
Returns the month of the year for date/datetime. Example: extract(month from timestamp '2007-02-03 09:00:00')/extract(month from date '2007-02-03') result:2
Returns minute for timestamp. Example: date_part('minute',timestamp '2014-03-08 12:20:19') result: 20
Returns the second for timestamp. Example:date_part(second, timestamp '2014-03-08 12:20:19') result: 19
Returns the ISO week of the year for date/dateTime. The value ranges from 1 to 53. Example: date_part('week',date '2014-03-08')/date_part('week',timestamp '2007-02-03 09:00:00') result: 10
Returns the quarter of the year for date/dateTime. Example: quarter(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00')/quarter(date '2014-03-08') result: 1.
Returns day of the month for date/datetime. Example: day(timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00')/day(date '2014-03-08') result: 3
Returns the millisecond for the second for dateTime. Example: EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM TIMESTAMP '2014-03-08 12:20:19.344') result : 344
Returns the month name based on the given date/datetime. Example: to_char(timestamp '2014-08-08 08:00:00.000', 'Month') result: August
Displays Current date and time. This function equivalent to current_timestamp.
Returns the name of the week day for date/dateTime. Example: trim(to_char(date '2007-02-03')/(timestamp '2007-02-03 09:00:00)) result: 'saturday'

Returns the ISO day of the week for date/datetime. The value ranges from 0(sunday) to 6 (saturday). Example: (date_part('dow',timestamp '2007-02-03 09:00:00')/(date_part('dow',date '2007-02-03')) result:6
Return year for date/dateTime. Example: date_part(year, timestamp '2014-03-08 09:00:00')/date_part(year, date '2014-03-08') result: 2014

Inside IF we will use AND. performs a logical conjunction on two expressions. In 'column' paramter we will 'drag column'. In 'condition' parameter provide conditions like (>,= etc.,like). In 'value' parameter provide condition value. In 'moreconditions' parameter we will use nested 'OR' condition , 'AND' conditions. Example: CASE WHEN 'Washington' like '%sh%' AND 'Washington' like 'W%' THEN 'return washington' else 'NotMatched' end

This case statement evaluates conditions from left to right and returns the corresponding result when the first condition is met. If none of the conditions are met, it returns the result specified after the 'ELSE' keyword. Here's an example:CASE WHEN Quantity > 30 THEN 'The quantity is greater than 30' ELSE 'The quantity is under 30' END In this example, if the quantity is greater than 30, it returns 'The quantity is greater than 30'. Otherwise, it returns 'The quantity is under 30'.
Returns from statement_list when condition gets fail.We will use ELSE inside case function. Example: CASE when 50 > 0 then 'true' else 'false'

function.signature: (case when ${column} ${condition} ${value} then ${conditiontrue} when ${elseIfcolumn} ${elseIfcondition} ${elseIfvalue} then ${elseIfconditiontrue} else ${conditionfalse} ${moreconditions} end)
function.description: Evaluates conditions and returns 'conditiontrue' if condition is true, otherwise return 'conditionfalse'. We will use nested condition inside else 'conditiontrue' parameter. Example:case when creditlim > 50000 then 'PLATINUM' else when (creditlim > = 50000) then 'GOLD' else 'SILVER' end

Inside IF we will use AND, OR conditions. Evaluates and returns 'conditiontrue' if condition is true, otherwise return 'conditionfalse'. In 'column' paramter we will drag column. In 'condition' parameter provide conditions like (>,= etc.,). In 'value' parameter provide value. In 'moreconditions' parameter we will use nested 'OR', 'AND' conditions. Instead of dragging column directly we will write expression in column parameter like 50 > 0 (Note : In such case don't provide anything in 'condition' parameter and 'value' parameter). Example : IF (creditlim > 50000) 'PLATINUM','SILVER'
Returns Expr1 if it is not null otherwise return expr2. Example : IFNULL(profit, 0)
Evaluates and returns 'conditiontrue' if condition is true, otherwise return 'conditionfalse'. Example : case when 'washington'like 'W%' then 'true' else 'false' end

Inside WHEN condition we will use ISNULL. Evalutes and returns 'Conditiontrue' if the expression contain Null. Example1 : CASE WHEN 1 ISNULL THEN Conditionfalse. Example2 : CASE WHEN NULL ISNULL THEN Conditiontrue
Evaluates and returns 'conditiontrue' if condition is false, otherwise returns 'conditionfalse'. We will use NOT inside IF. Example : NOT(500 > 1000) result :true

Inside IF we will use OR. Performs a logical disjunction on two expressions. In 'column' paramter we will drag column. In 'condition' parameter provide conditions like (>,= etc.,). In 'value' parameter provide value. In 'moreconditions' parameter we will use nested 'OR' , 'AND' functions. Example : CASE WHEN 'Washington' like '%sh%' OR 'Washington' like 'W%' THEN 'return washington' else 'NotMatched' end

Returns 'statement_list' when condition get satisfied . In column paramter we will drag column. In searchcondition parameter provide conditions like (>, =, IS Null etc .,). In value parameter provide value(Note : IS Null used in 'condition' parameter then don't provide anything in 'value' parameter). In moreconditions parameter we will use nested when conditions, Else condition . We will use WHEN inside CASE. Example1 : CASE WHEN 1 > 0 THEN 'one' else 'TWO'. Example2 : CASE WHEN 'Singapore' IS NULL THEN 'Singa' ELSE 'pore'. Example3 : CASE WHEN Washington like '%sh%' THEN 'return washington' else 'NotMatched'

function.signature: (CASE WHEN ${expr} IS NULL THEN '0' ELSE ${expr} end)
function.description: (CASE WHEN null IS NULL THEN '0' ELSE null end) would result in `'0'` because it follows the pattern where if the expression is not null, it returns the expression itself, otherwise it returns `0`.

This function compares 'actualValue' and 'expectedValue' based on the operator if condition succeeds then changes the 'actualValue' to 'assignValue' else 'elseValue'. Note:All Values should be in single quotes if you are typing the value.

This function is used for numeric values, compares 'actualValue' and 'expectedValue' based on the operator if condition succeeds then changes the 'actualValue' to 'assignValue' else 'elseValue'. Note:All Values should be in single quotes if you are typing the value.

This function compares 'actualValue' and 'compareValue' if both are not equal then changes the 'actualValue' to 'changeValue' other wise 'actualValue' remains same. Note:All Values should be in single quotes if you are typing the value.

CASE WHEN actualValue <> compareValue THEN changeValue ELSE actualValue END This SQL expression checks if `actualValue` is not equal to `compareValue`. If they're not equal, it updates `actualValue` to `changeValue`; otherwise, it keeps `actualValue` unchanged.

This function returns the first non-NULL expression among its arguments.If all expressions evaluate to null, then the COALESCE function returns null. NOTE:expressions should be in single quotes if you are typing value.Example:COALESCE(20,0) results: 20, COALESCE(null,0) results: 0
The NULLIF function returns the null value if the two arguments are equal; otherwise, it returns the value of the first argument. Example:NULLIF(123,123) results: null, NULLIF(123,129) results: 123
Converts a JSON null value to a SQL NULL value.
Parses the first argument as a JSON string and returns the value of the element pointed to by the path in the second argument.
Checks the validity of a JSON document. If the input string is a valid JSON document or a NULL, the output is NULL (i.e. no error).
Interprets an input string as a JSON document, producing a VARIANT value.
Converts a VARIANT value to a string containing the JSON representation of the value. If the input is NULL, the result is also NULL.
A special version of PARSE_JSON that returns a NULL value if an error occurs during parsing.

Cast function converts one dataType to another datatype. Note:All Values should be in single quotes if user provide's value.Example: CAST('2019-03-22 17:34:03.000' AS char) result:2019-03-22 17:34:03.000
Converts value to char type. NOTE:field should be in single quotes if you are typing manually.Example:CAST(456 as VARCHAR(3)) result:'456'
Converts the number to string
Converts the date to string
Converts the time to string
Converts the datetime to string
Converts the time to string

function.signature: 1* CAST((case when length(${column})=0 then null else ${column} end) AS BIGINT)
function.description: This function is used to convert character based integer value to integer type.(format is not required)Example:CAST('456' as BIGINT)) result:456
This function is used to convert character based decimal value to decimal type.(format is not required)Example:CAST('456.34' as DECIMAL(5,2))) result:456.34
This function is used to convert character based date value to date type.(format is not required)Example:CAST('2018-08-30' as DATE)) result:2018-08-30
This function is used to convert character based dateTime value to dateTime type.(format is not required)Example:CAST('2018-08-30 10:15:30' as TIMESTAMP)) result:2018-08-30 10:15:30
This function is used to convert character based time value to time type.(format is not required) Example:CAST('10:15:30' as TIME)) result:10:15:30
Computes the factorial of its input. The input argument must be an integer expression in the range of 0 to 33.
Returns 0 if its argument is null; otherwise, returns its argument
Extracts Hour from date time
Extracts Minute from date time
Extracts seconds from date time
Builds a string by repeating the input for the specified number of times.
